specific_location: Fox Ranch. On the south bank of the Arickaree River, a few hundred feet west of Yuma County Road U.
description: A "spot-breasted thrush," gray-brown above, paler and gray-white below. First seen mainly facing away from me; the bird was a uniform cold gray-brown above, and, to the extent that I could see the face, I was struck by the large black eye against a cold brown-gray background.  Angling around for a look at the bird in profile and then nearly head-on, I got very good looks at the face, throat and breast; I noted the brown-gray to gray-white background color of the throat, breast, and face. The uppermost part of the breast (i.e., directly below the unmarked gray-white throat) tended toward more of a weak buff-gray background color, which is normal for Gray-cheeked Thrush. Otherwise, the bird's face, throat and breast lacked warm or buff tones altogether.  The visible primaries were long and nearly black. The bird imparted a strong-legged, long-winged appearance overall. Its upperparts were uniform in color; when it finally flew directly away from me, I got great looks at the tail-to-back contrast, of which there was none. The bird was alert during the entire period of observation.Behaviors: The bird was perched in a low willow for 10 minutes. During the period of observation, it moved around 3 or 4 times within the willow. Its movements were probably related to my close approach. The bird did not call while in the willow. After 10 minutes it flew away, and it did not give a flight call as it departed. At no time did the bird flick its tail up then lower it.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
